stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Indian astronomer Aryabhata studied the motions of celestial bodies.
  • He proposed that Earth rotates on its own axis, causing the apparent daily motion of the stars.
  • Aryabhata published his rotation theory in 499 CE.
stem: The student wants to specify when Aryabhata published his rotation theory. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Aryabhata, who studied the motions of celestial bodies, proposed a theory about Earth’s rotation.
Aryabhata was an Indian astronomer who studied the motions of celestial bodies.
An Indian astronomer who studied celestial bodies, Aryabhata theorized that Earth rotates on its own axis, causing the apparent daily motion of the stars.
In 499 CE, Aryabhata published his theory that Earth rotates on its own axis, causing the apparent daily motion of the stars.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The sentence specifies when Aryabhata published his rotation theory, indicating that it was published in 499 CE.

Choice A is incorrect. While the sentence notes that Aryabhata proposed a rotation theory, it doesn’t specify when the theory was published. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence identifies Aryabhata as an astronomer; it doesn’t specify when he published his theory. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence describes Aryabhata’s theory; it doesn’t specify when the theory was published.
